item.title: Murdochs’ aversion to big buyouts meets its match in the streaming age
item.description: Fox’s shares dip following news of $22bn acquisition of Roku

item.title: The least bad exit from Trump’s war of choice
item.description: To ensure deal succeeds, the US president must contain Israel’s prime minister

item.title: AI giants are learning a hard lesson about pricing power
item.description: Anthropic, until Friday’s White House move, looked like one of the more rationally valued companies in its peer group

item.title: Cutting access to Anthropic’s Mythos is a gift to China
item.description: Washington’s suspension just strengthened Beijing’s pitch for its own competing AI models

item.title: Me and my exoskeleton: the rise of wearable robotics
item.description: Lighter and more affordable devices give users a battery-powered spring in their step

item.title: Global imbalances need not lead to trade wars
item.description: The world’s benign arithmetic will turn malign if countries do not adjust

item.title: A war without victors
item.description: Trump’s deal reflects the failure of the military option in Iran

item.title: Social media ban reveals Starmer’s coherence problem
item.description: If 16-year-olds are old enough to vote, they should have access to the same information environment as adults

item.title: SpaceX: no crying at the casino
item.description: Plus, Magnificent 7 RIP

item.title: The inescapable return of an old financial framework
item.description: Understanding both the ‘sources and uses’ of investible capital will become paramount as competition for funds rises

item.title: Lessons from Ireland’s Revolut revolution
item.description: The fintech’s model is proving more suitable for international scaling than the often outdated systems of legacy rivals

item.title: Britain’s return to the EU is only a matter of time
item.description: There are no alternatives the people would favour. Increasingly, they know it

item.title: The populist case for ending easy money now
item.description: Why Kevin Warsh’s first big act at the Fed should be a rate hike

item.title: ‘Time kills a deal’ more than ever in a slow housing market
item.description: In prime London, the ratio of homes under offer to fall-throughs has not been this high since the 2008 financial crisis. Imminent conveyancing reform needs to be radical

item.title: AI holds the key to faster battery tech development
item.description: Opportunity to transform materials discovery could outweigh risks of high energy consumption

item.title: Don’t ask Burnham what he’d do as PM. I’m not sure he’s in the loop
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at the work of Rutherford Hall, critical communications strategist

item.title: ‘Can a machine do this job?’ is the wrong question
item.description: By shifting work to the consumer, AI will usher in a self-service economy

item.title: Manchester is a triumph of private enterprise
item.description: ‘Soft left’ Labour MPs backing Andy Burnham might have learned the wrong lessons from the city’s revival

item.title: Kevin Warsh enters the arena
item.description: The new Federal Reserve Chair will be judged on his integrity and ability

item.title: Wes Streeting: This is what progressive capitalism should look like
item.description: Labour must be as focused on wealth creation as we are on wealth distribution

item.title: Tabletop game titans offer two ways to play the nerd economy
item.description: While Games Workshop investors have tripled their money, Hasbro shareholders have seen barely any increase

item.title: How will history judge today’s chief executives?
item.description: Leading through the AI storm is shaping up to be a test for the ages

item.title: Trump’s many unhappy returns
item.description: No kings. No The Rock. No gratitude.

item.title: Europe’s fighter jet fiasco
item.description: The collapse of Paris and Berlin’s joint venture raises the question of whether either country can do defence alone

item.title: Chart of the Week: US headline inflation matters to consumers
item.description: The headline annual inflation rate hit 4.2%, the highest since April 2023
